macro_segments.title: Introduction to Daf 115 and the Haggadah Structure

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and schematic of the Haggadah
Progression through Kadesh, Karpas, Urchatz and key instructions

macro_segments.title: The Gemara's Discussion of Maror at the Beginning: Intent and Two Dippings

micro_segments.title: Mishna permits using Maror for Karpas; Gemara explores the halakhic basis
Reish Lakish's position: Mitzvos require intent; why Maror upfront is allowed
Alternative view: Mitzvos do not require intent; using Maror for two dippings
Pedagogical note on Halailah hazeh kulo Maror and symbolic ordering
Discussion of Braisa on eating Maror without intent and in divided eatings
Debate on Rabbi Yosei and whether Maror was fully discharged upfront

macro_segments.title: Blessings for Two Dippings of Maror: Rav Huna vs. Rav Chisda

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna: separate blessings (Borei Pri ha'Adama first, al Achilat Maror later)
Rav Chisda's objection: can't make al Achilat Maror after already eating Maror
Rav Chisda's solution: both blessings upfront, then eat Maror twice without blessing
Practice in Surya followed Rav Huna; Rav Sheshes' son followed Rav Chisda
Halakha ruled like Rav Chisda
Tosafot's explanation: Tzrichos Kavana but can't say blessing after eating Maror already

macro_segments.title: Mitzvos at Different Levels Do Not Combine: Matzah (D'Oraita) and Maror (D'Rabbanan)

micro_segments.title: Principle: Mitzvos negate one another only when they differ in biblical vs. rabbinic status
Hillel wrapped Pesach, Matzah, and Maror together based on al matzos umerorim yochluhu
Rabbis disagree: yochluhu hu implies separate consumption of each
Rav Ashi reinterprets: Rabbis mean you can eat separately, not that you must
Current halakha: Matzah d'Oraita and Maror d'Rabbanan require separate eating
Procedure: bless on Matzah, eat Matzah; bless on Maror, eat Maror; then eat together as zecher le-Hillel

macro_segments.title: Shekual in Liquid and Netilas Yadayim Before Karpas

micro_segments.title: Rav Elazar and Rabbi Oshaya: anything dipped in liquid requires washing hands
Rav Papa infers: Maror must be immersed in Charoses to counteract Kappa
What is Kappa: possibly a bug or dangerous sap in bitter herbs
Tosafot: Danger is more serious than prohibition; immersion in Charoses protects health
Rav Papa: don't leave Maror immersed in Charoses lest the spices negate its bitterness
Discussion of whether hand-washing is required for both dippings (Karpas and Motzi)
Rav Papa: washing hands twice needed due to potential neglect (Hesach ha-Daas) during Haggadah recitation

macro_segments.title: Tasting Requirement for Maror and Proper Consumption

micro_segments.title: Rava: swallowing Matzah whole fulfills; swallowing Maror whole does not
Reason: Maror requires tasting the bitter flavor; Matzah does not require taste
If Matzah and Maror swallowed together whole: Matzah fulfilled but Maror not
If wrapped in something inedible: even Matzah is not consumed

macro_segments.title: Individual Seder Plates and Removal of the Table

micro_segments.title: Rav Shimi bar Ashi: Matzah, Maror, and Charoses must be before each person
Table removal debate: Rav Huna rules all items before Seder leader only
Purpose of removing the table: to provoke children to ask Mah Nishtana
Abaye's question when table was removed: why before we've eaten?
Rabbah's response: you've exempted us from needing to recite Mah Nishtana formally
Tosafot: removing table is only partial exemption from Mah Nishtana; formal text still required

topical_tags.term: Hillel vs. Rabbis (wrapping Matzah and Maror together)
D'Oraita vs. D'Rabbanan (biblical vs. rabbinic obligation)
Mitzvos Mevatlos Zu Es Zu (mitzvos negate one another)
Shekual Ba-Mashke (dipping in liquid / hand washing)
Seder plate and removal of Shulchan (table)
Rav Huna vs. Rav Chisda (blessings procedure)
Al Achilat Maror (blessing over bitter herbs)
